title: Improve search UX, list rendering interactions, and visual polish
description: Enhance the search experience with better loading states, result count display, empty state messaging, and smoother transitions. Improve component list rendering with better hover/active states, consistent spacing, and scroll behavior. Apply overall visual polish: refine shadows, border-radius usage, animation timings, and micro-interactions following ui-ux-pro-max guidelines. Reorganize the internal code structure for clarity without changing the Editor.Panel.extend() pattern.

criteria: Search shows loading skeleton or spinner during IPC query (replacing blank state during 200ms debounce + IPC round trip)
Search results display count badge (e.g., '12 results') in the results area
Empty search state shows helpful message with icon instead of blank area
Search result hover/active animations use 150-300ms duration with transform/opacity only (no width/height animations)
Component bar scroll behavior improved: scroll snap or smooth scrolling with visible scroll indicators
Consistent border-radius usage: small elements use --radius-sm, cards use --radius-md, modals use --radius-lg
Shadow usage refined: subtle shadows for cards (--shadow-sm), elevated shadows for modals (--shadow-lg)
All CSS transitions use transform and opacity only for 60fps performance (no layout-triggering properties)
Internal code sections clearly delineated with comment blocks: CSS section, HTML section, bindings, lifecycle, search methods, component methods, settings methods, IPC handlers
convergence.verification: Type in search input and observe loading state, result count, and transition animations. Scroll component bar and verify smooth behavior. Check code organization in panel/index.js.

files.target: style property (CSS animations, transitions, loading states)
files.change: Add loading skeleton/spinner CSS, refine hover/active transitions to 150-300ms using transform/opacity only, add scroll-snap styles, polish shadow and border-radius usage

files.target: template property (HTML for loading states and empty states)
files.change: Add loading indicator element in search results area, add empty state message template, add result count display element

files.target: onInput2 and search result rendering methods
files.change: Show loading state when search starts, display result count, show empty state message when no results, smooth transition between states
Improve scroll behavior with smooth scrolling, add scroll indicators for overflow content
Add section comment blocks to clearly delineate CSS, HTML, bindings, lifecycle, and method groups without changing functionality

implementation: Add CSS for loading skeleton animation: @keyframes shimmer with linear-gradient background-position animation, .loading-skeleton class
Add CSS for search result count badge, empty state container with centered icon and message
Refine all CSS transitions: audit every transition property, ensure only transform/opacity are animated (replace any width/height/margin transitions), set durations to 150-300ms range
Add scroll-snap-type: x mandatory to component bar (#coms) with scroll-snap-align: start on items; add scrollbar styling for consistency
Update onInput2 method: show loading skeleton when search starts (before debounce fires), show result count after results arrive, show empty state message when results array is empty
Update search result rendering: add subtle entrance animation (opacity 0->1, translateY 4px->0) for smoother appearance
Polish shadow usage: ensure cards use --shadow-sm, elevated elements use --shadow-md, modals use --shadow-lg consistently
Polish border-radius usage: small interactive elements (buttons, badges) use --radius-sm, content cards use --radius-md, modals/dialogs use --radius-lg
Add section comment blocks throughout panel/index.js: /* ========== CSS DESIGN TOKENS ========== */, /* ========== HTML TEMPLATE ========== */, etc.
Test all animations respect prefers-reduced-motion (existing media query should cascade, verify new animations are covered)

reference.pattern: Existing cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) easing and translateY hover patterns
reference.examples: ui-ux-pro-max: duration-timing 150-300ms, transform-performance (use transform/opacity only), loading-states (skeleton screens or spinners), content-jumping (reserve space for async content)

rationale.chosen_approach: Incremental UX improvements within existing DOM structure: loading states, result counts, empty states, animation polish, and code organization comments
alternatives_considered: Virtual scrolling for large result lists (over-engineering for typical result counts < 50)
Full layout restructure with CSS Grid (risk of breaking docked panel sizing)
Extract UI rendering into separate module files (user chose to keep single-file)
rationale.tradeoffs: Loading states add minor complexity to search flow but significantly improve perceived performance

manual_checks: Search shows loading indicator during query, then transitions smoothly to results
Result count displays correctly (matches actual result count)
Empty search state shows helpful message, not blank area
Component bar scrolls smoothly with visible scroll indicators
All animations disabled when prefers-reduced-motion is set
Panel functions correctly at default 450x120 size and when resized
success_metrics: Zero layout-triggering animation properties (no width/height/margin animations)
Code sections clearly identifiable via comment blocks
